technical_knowledge.q8_complex_feature: I built a Flutter feature that integrates with an ESP32-based IoT device to enable real-time ordering. Users can pre-configure four hardware buttons in the app, and pressing a button automatically orders the assigned item. The implementation required managing asynchronous communication, device connectivity, and reliable state synchronization between the app and hardware.
technical_knowledge.q9_stateful_vs_bloc:
I decide based on scope, complexity, and state-sharing requirements.

StatefulWidget is suitable when the state is local to a single widget or small UI interactions (e.g., toggling a switch, animating a component) that don’t need to be shared elsewhere.
BLoC (or any centralized state management) is used when the feature involves complex logic, asynchronous operations, or shared state across multiple screens (e.g., fetching data from an API, handling user sessions, or reacting to hardware events).

technical_knowledge.q12_const_sizedbox:
const SizedBox() vs SizedBox() comes down to compile-time vs runtime instantiation:

const SizedBox()
The widget is created at compile-time and reused whenever the same const instance appears.
Flutter can skip rebuilding it because it knows the widget is immutable and identical across rebuilds.
This reduces memory usage and avoids unnecessary build() calls.
SizedBox() (without const)
The widget is created at runtime every time the build method runs.
Even if nothing changed, Flutter sees it as a new instance, which may trigger unnecessary rebuilds of child widgets.
technical_knowledge.q13_friday_bug:
Step 1: assess the bug — figure out how critical it is and who it affects.
Step 2: Alert the team and stakeholders so everyone knows the situation.
Step 3: Reproduce the issue in staging to understand the root cause safely.
Step 4: Apply a hotfix or rollback if needed, then test thoroughly.
Step 5: Deploy the fix and monitor production to ensure it’s resolved.
Step 6: Document the incident and share lessons to prevent it from happening again.

how_you_work.q14_architecture_disagreement:
Situation: While implementing a new feature, I noticed the proposed architecture differed from the existing project structure.

My perspective: I recommended sticking to the established approach because having a consistent architecture across the app ensures maintainability, scalability, and easier debugging. It also makes future improvements simpler, and new developers can quickly understand the project — once they grasp one feature, they can navigate the rest.

Outcome: The team agreed to follow the existing structure, which kept the codebase consistent, made onboarding easier, and allowed us to enhance features and fix bugs more efficiently.
how_you_work.q15_staying_current: I stay current with Flutter and Dart by following multiple channels. I regularly read the official Flutter and Dart release notes, check the Flutter Dev blog, and monitor GitHub discussions for best practices. I also participate in Flutter communities like GDG , follow tech talks and YouTube tutorials, and experiment with new features in small side projects to understand them hands-on. This combination of reading, community engagement, and practical experimentation helps me keep up-to-date and write modern, efficient Flutter code.
how_you_work.q16_code_review: My approach to code review focuses on clarity, simplicity, and consistency with the planned architecture. I look for code that meets the intended functionality while being clean, readable, and easy to maintain, avoiding unnecessary complexity. A good PR follows the established architecture, implements the feature efficiently, has clear commit messages, and includes well-tested, understandable code that anyone on the team can pick up and extend in the future.
how_you_work.q17_ai_usage: I used AI tools, specifically ChatGPT, to help with a few aspects of the assessment. For example, I used it to analyze long code files (+500 lines), which sped up understanding and debugging. I also leveraged it to generate clear, well-organized explanations and summaries. Additionally, when there were two competing approaches or solutions, I used it to evaluate which option was better and understand the reasoning behind each choice. Overall, it helped me work more efficiently and make more informed decisions.
